All right, JB here in the house. Listen, 10 tonalities. Remember, we use these tonalities again and again as we move our prospects down the straight line. If you master them in the beginning, in those first 30 seconds when you're going to use a lot of them right there, they'll just keep duplicating themselves and repeating themselves over and over again. They become so easy. Never forget this one fact. There's four levels of learning. Right? You start off at the lowest level of learning when you are unconsciously incompetent. That means like you weren't even aware that tonality was an important function of communication. Like you were just unaware, flying blind. Then you learned a bit about the straight line perhaps and you said, oh my God, wow. There's this tonality. It sounds really good, man. Wow, does that guy sound good? I don't know how to use it, but boy, I should probably learn that. So your awareness is heightened. That's called being consciously incompetent. From there, you said, I'm going to start in a straight line. And here we are right now. You're in the process of becoming consciously competent if you're not already so. In other words, you can use tonality now. You can apply these tonalities. You're getting better. I'm sure you all because it works really fast, but you're not at that highest level of learning, which is being unconsciously competent, which means you can now do this stuff with your eyes closed. That's where you're going to get. And the only way you get this through practice, repetition and drilling. And remember breaking up into groups, having a partner, analyzing what someone else is saying, and then you're going to be able to get to the next level. And then say, Hey, when you want it to sound scarce, you're moving your body this way and you were certain you moved your body that way. Break up into groups. Trust me, it's huge. It's so important and you really can't get these little hacks or shortcuts into mustering tonality that are giving you problems. All right. Bottom line is this tonality is like, it's like the salt on your food. It makes everything you say sound better, more impactful. You want to get rich in this world. You want to live a great life. You want to be a top producer. You can't do it without the right tonality. You know what?
